questions.question: What fraction of valid question, answer, comment, and linked-question records declared by the canonical public Stack Exchange-shaped starter fixture are materialized as normalized evidence items with matching source identity and policy-allowed source routes?

population.subject: question, answer, comment, and linked-question records declared by the canonical public Stack Exchange-shaped starter fixture
population.definition: one unique valid question, answer, question or answer comment, or linked-question record with non-empty source identity, evidence-bearing text or title, and a policy-allowed public StackOverflow route in the complete current fixture
population.inclusion_rule: every valid unique source record in the complete canonical public starter fixture
population.exclusion_rule: record text, tags, entities, accepted-answer or score interpretation, generated index and graph objects, claims, answer packets, eval outcomes, live StackOverflow content, readiness, proof, and runtime state

measurements.authority_ceiling: Reports only the fraction of valid source records in the canonical public starter fixture that the current normalizer materializes with matching StackOverflow identities and policy-allowed source routes at one source revision; it does not establish content completeness or correctness, live-source coverage, accepted-answer truth, score meaning, claim extraction, index or graph quality, retrieval or answer quality, connector readiness, eval success, proof verdicts, or runtime health.
